sections.title: Talking to Awusah after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Many salutations to you sir, have you made any progress in uncovering the reasons for our curse yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am working on it.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Please try your best, that water salesman is not doing much good for the economy of our town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I meant to do again?

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Look around this town, see if you can uncover any information to why we were cursed and how the curse might be lifted. I suspect that it might be because a priest of Saradomin died while staying at our town, but

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: that isn't much help in getting the curse lifted and it is possible I'm wrong anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't really had the time to investigate.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Well I hope somebody can find the time soon, the despair of the people of this town increases daily.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Good day to you young man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to find out the cause of this town's curse.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Oh that's something which no one but the gods and the river spirits seem to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: River spirits, what are they?

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: They're the guardians of the source of the river Elid, they'll know of our curse, seeing as it's related to water we bring from the river. It's said that nothing happens on the river that they don't know about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So would it be possible for me to find these spirits and ask them what is going on?

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Ahh now the spirits are something which no one from here have seen in a good long time, certainly not in my lifetime, I don't think even in my own grandfather's lifetime.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Most the younger people of this town don't even remember that the spirits even exist.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Here I have something around here somewhere which may be of interest to you.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Aha yes here it is, the ballad of Jareesh. This tells of a previous quest to try and find the spirits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you very much for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things?

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Lousy, young people have no respect for their elders, there's too many confusing newfangled magic carpets and snake charmers around these days and now we don't even have any water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I hope things pick up for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to find out the cause of this town's curse.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Well with the moral standards of this town lately, I'm hardly surprised.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what have they been up to?

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Nothing someone like you would understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Be Careful in here and don't touch anything! There's some old and valuable artefacts around here. Careless outsiders.. you're always coming in and making a mess of things!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok calm down, I'm not going to touch anything.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Yes, good don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this building?

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: This is our local shrine to She.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by She?

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Such ignorance! I suppose I should come to expect it. People are forgetting the old ways of the desert around here. But then.. What do those bureaucratic fools in Menaphos expect! If they're going to take so long to

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: send us a new priestess of She. Do you know we haven't had one for five years now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errm you still haven't told me what you mean by She.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: She! Otherwise know as Elidinis, Goddess of growth and fertility, Lady of the river, Wife of Tumeken, Flower of the desert, Mother of Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That all sounds very confusing, I might just call her Elidinis for short.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is that key on display?

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: That's a very valuable artefact. Keep your hands off it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ..I just wanted to know what it did.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: That's the key to the holy chambers of the great and mighty Spirits of the Elid, and it's staying right there. There are other holy items about this building and keep your hands off them too, but most definitely keep your

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: hands off the key. People already meddle too much with the affairs of the shrine.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Many salutations to you. What brings you to our little town?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am an adventurer in search of quests.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Hmm, well that could be quite fortuitous. We really could do with some help here. Our town seems to have come under a curse, and we are desperate to get it removed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A curse? What sort of curse?

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Well first of all our fountain, which has served us for many years, suddenly dried up. We then started transporting water from the river, but what we carried would always have completely dried up by the time we

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: got it back to the village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you do for water now?

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Well it's tough. There is a guy who calls himself Ali the carter down in the market. He's from Pollnivneach, so the curse doesn't seem to have affected him. He's got a cart full of water which he's selling to the people here.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Unfortunately in typical Pollnivneach style he's taking the opportunity to sell the water at outrageous prices because he knows we are desperate. Oh and Rokuh has developed something quite remarkable, called a choc ice

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: which is helping people stay cool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea how you got this curse?

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Well we think it was because we were negligent in our duty to protect a priest of Saradomin. He came to us a few weeks ago from the north and we welcomed him into our town. He taught us much of the wisdom of

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Saradomin. Then about a week ago, we found him dead by the river. It seems unlikely that it is coincidence we were then cursed. If you look around the town you might be able to find out more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok I will have a look around and see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Good luck to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear of your plight, but I must be going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear of your plight, but I must be going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Well we've not fallen on the best of times here, but there are a few shops to look around and we have someone selling a marvelous new invention called a choc ice..

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Oh, and there's the old temple if you're interested in a bit of history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm passing through on my way to Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Oh you don't want to go there. I hear rumours of plagues and the like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the warning, but I might go and investigate anyway.

lines.speaker: Ali the Carter
lines.text: Water, water get your fresh water here, 1000 coins a waterskin, a bargain in these difficult times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's extortionate! Why so much?

lines.speaker: Ali the Carter
lines.text: Well it's the cheapest water you'll find in this town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you happen to know anything about the curse?

lines.speaker: Ali the Carter
lines.text: Yes the people round here can't seem to get the water themselves. It's an ideal business opportunity for one such as myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It does seem to be slightly exploiting their unfortunate situation.

lines.speaker: Ali the Carter
lines.text: I don't see it like that. Without me they'd be in even more of a mess. I'm doing them a favour and in true Pollnivneachian spirit making a profit along the way.

lines.speaker: Rokuh
lines.text: Come one, come all, buy my amazing choc ice invention here! Chocolate on the outside. Iced cream on the inside. Oh I also have some chocolate left over from making choc ices which I'm selling too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to find out the cause of this town's curse.

lines.speaker: Rokuh
lines.text: I really have no idea. It's most regrettable. Fortunately my innovation with my choc ice invention is helping people stay at least slightly cool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I am trying to find the cause of the curse on your town.

lines.speaker: Garai
lines.text: What curse is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you know? The curse that has stopped your fountain working and made all the water dry up when you bring it back from the river?

lines.speaker: Garai
lines.text: Ohh that's because of a curse? I didn't think of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So any ideas about the cause of the curse?

lines.speaker: Garai
lines.text: Absolutely none.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I am trying to find the cause of the curse on your town.

lines.speaker: Nkuku
lines.text: It's terrible isn't it. Surely it is because we let the holy Saradominist priest die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I am trying to find the cause of the curse on your town.

lines.speaker: Usi
lines.text: Well I think that's what everyone wants to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I am trying to find the cause of the curse on your town.

lines.speaker: Habibah/Meskhenet/Zahra
lines.text: Ah I see. Awusah has enlisted your help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes he has.

lines.speaker: Habibah/Meskhenet/Zahra
lines.text: Well, I really don't know why it might have happened. Maybe Ghaslor has some ideas. He's very wise and old and is the town's archivist. He lives in the northeast corner of town.

sections.title: Habibah, Meskhenet, and Zahra after talking to Nirrie and Hallak

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard the people of Nardah threw their statue of Elidinis away.

lines.speaker: Habibah/Meskhenet/Zahra
lines.text: Yes that's correct. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found out that action seems to have caused this town's curse.

lines.speaker: Habibah/Meskhenet/Zahra
lines.text: Well, I guess that would be logical conclusion. I don't really know where the statue was thrown. Awusah might know.

sections.title: Attempting to open the door without the robes of Elidinis equipped

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's odd. I wonder what I can have forgotten.

sections.title: Talking to the Spirits of the Elid

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: What brings thee to our domain? It has been a long time since

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: we have seen any of your kind. Thou art not even

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: one of the desert peoples!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow how do you finish each others sentences like that?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: We are a gestalt entity. We each have our own body, but our minds

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: are united and we all think as one. Well except when Hallak doth leaveth

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: the toilet seat up.

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: That's the way it's meant to be!

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: Ok we're a mostly gestalt entity. We each have own body, but

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: our minds are united, except for some little parts which aren't. We mostly

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: think as one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't really have the same ring to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give Nardah its water back!

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: This little one doth come blundering into our abode making demands, with no thought for

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: diplomacy or decorum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you calling little?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: You are little in power compared to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just tell me what it takes to get Nardah some water.

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: Thou shouldst calm down, getting worked up like that will not help thee.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me what I need and I'll leave.

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: Well maybe we're not that desperate for thee to leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look you don't want me to get annoyed.

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: That's a shame because thou art annoyed already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes I'm annoyed, now give them their water.

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: You know thou art really not skilled at diplomacy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't time for this nonsense.

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: Well we know ye mortals are always in a rush with

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: your very busy little lives. We'd better let thee get on. We

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: enjoyed thy visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it wasn't so easy getting in here. Why have you made it so tough? You must really not like guests.

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: We are powerful spirits with direct contact to the great goddess Elidinis, we do not need to be bothered by the trifles of

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: each and every human. This way only those of much resource and intellect, may gain audience with us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I come as an emissary from the people of Nardah. Their fountain has dried up and water they bring from the river dries up. What advice can you give on this matter?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: How unusual, that the one who would come unto us on this issue isn't even from Nardah. Yes, it was indeed we

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: who have withheld their water from them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was this due to the death of the Saradominist priest as the towns people think?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: No that also was our doing!

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: He did not respect our ways and the ways of the desert! He did not respect the ways of Elidinis!

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: He came preaching the ways of Saradomin. Which is all well and good, but he also did turneth them from the path of the desert gods, especially Elidinis. Elidinis is happy to share the worship of followers, indeed Elidinis

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: was allied with Saradomin many ages past. Saradominist mages even gave us those golems you met as a diplomatic gesture, so you can see we have been on good terms. However this is an outrage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you've stopped finishing each others sentences!

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: That seems to be something which happens when we get emotional

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: We are

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: calmer now though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't many other towns not follow Elidinis though? Why pick on Nardah specifically?

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: This is the truth, but there is more to the story than that. The answers

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: lie in the story of Nardarine. Wouldst thou likest to hear the telling of this tale? It is not a short one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I would like to hear this story.

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: Nardarine came from a town towards the north side of the desert which does not exist today. This was in the third age in the time thou callest

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: the God Wars. Her town was once controlled by Saradomin, but there was a great battle there between Saradominist and Zamorakian forces which pretty much levelled the town

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: to the ground. Most of the human citizens perished. Nardarine had the wisdom to escape into the desert before the fighting got really serious and thus survived. However she found herself wandering lost and alone in

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: the desert. She prayed to Saradomin for help, but Saradomin was too concerned with the loss of a strategic outpost to worry about the fate of one small human. Nardarine had wandered towards the realm of

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: the Menaphite God Pantheon. Elidinis heard the prayers of Nardarine, she took pity on the wandering human and came unto her with water. 'Oh great goddess' exclaimed Nardarine 'I wish not to be involved more in

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: the wars of the gods for they have caused me so much hardship already, I wish only for a quiet life somewhere'. Elidinis replied 'I will grant this unto thee on the condition that thou swearest to forever pay fealty and

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: homage to me and I grant this also unto thine descendents as long as they too will pay an oath of fealty and homage to me'. Nardarine said 'If you would grant me a safe home, with a

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: supply of food and water, then I would surely do this for it is more than any other God would seem to do for me'. Then Elidinis made

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: a spring of water appear at Nardarines feet and brought stone from below the sand so that she

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: might have something to build with. She called forth camels which have many uses and taste good in kebabs. She then provided Nardarine with a statuette in her image to worship. Nardarine built up her home. Over

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: time more refugees from the God Wars found Nardarine's home. Nardarine let them stay with her as long as they did make the same oath as her. Hence the town of Nardah was born and Nardarine lived the rest

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: of her days in the peacefulness that she craved. The oath was passed down the generations for many ages, but now seemeth to be forgotten. Now maybe thou canst see why we are not best pleased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That does make some sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything they can do to get their fountain working again?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: I do not know. The Saradominist priest persuaded the people of Nardah to throw away a statuette of Elidinis which was in the temple. As long as they do not have that image of Elidinis to pray to, then we must consider

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: them turned away from her and the curse shalt stand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's all very interesting, I wonder what the people of Nardah will think of this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What manner of beings are you?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: We are the spirits of the river Elid, many ages ago we used to be mindless wandering spirits, with little mind or purpose, we were here even in the first age. Then great Elidinis came to the desert and bound us to her

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: river, we've

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: become one with the river and over the centuries become full of the power of Elidinis that was in her river. We are now her trusted guardians of the Elid, we can control the river and anything which happens

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: near the river we know about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more of Elidinis.

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: Elidinis is one of a group of gods, known as the Menaphite Pantheon. She is the wife of their leader Tumeken. She is the Menaphite goddess of growth and fertility, and goddess of the river.

sections.title: Talking to the Spirits of the Elid again after learning about the statuette

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: Thou art back already?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give Nardah its water back!

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: : I thought we had been through this already. You are not in any position to make such demands. As long as they do not have their statuette of Elidinis to pray to, then we

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: : must consider them turned away from her and the curse shalt stand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it's nice and cool here.

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: : That it is, praise the power of Elidinis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did the townspeople need to do again?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: : As long as they do not have their statuette of Elidinis to pray to, then we

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: : must consider them turned away from her and the curse shalt stand.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Many salutations to you sir, have you made any progress in uncovering the reasons for our curse yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have made some progress. It is the doing of the water spirits at the source of the river.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: The Spirits of the Elid? I wasn't even sure if they weren't some old wives tale. Why would they do such a thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you by any chance throw away a statuette of the goddess Elidinis recently?

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Oh that old thing. Yeah, the priest of Saradomin said we should get rid of that. He said we should become followers of Saradomin, and therefore we wouldn't need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the spirits killed him for imparting that piece of advice about the statuette.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Gasps!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently this town was brought about by Elidinis on the promise that its people would follow her. People here have been waning in their belief of Elidinis for a while now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems the casting away of the statue was the final straw.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Ohh that's not so good if only we hadn't thrown the statue away, we wouldn't have this water problem then. He was a very convincing priest though. He worked many wonders, for example if he prayed hard enough

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: he became impervious to arrows. The way of Saradomin did seem very appealing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh but that's easy, I can pray to protect myself from arrows too.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Then truly you are very powerful too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you throw the statuette?

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Ohh there's a strange deep crevice in the desert to the west of here. We threw it down there. I don't know if it's lost forever or not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it's worth a look anyway.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: If you could retrieve it for us we'll be very grateful. Even if it doesn't give us our water back, maybe it'll calm Shiratti down, she was not pleased at it being thrown away and she's been grumpy ever since.

sections.title: Talking to Awusah the Mayor again

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Have you had any luck retrieving our statuette yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but don't give up hope yet.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Good luck, may Sarad.. errm I mean Elidinis guide you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you say you threw it again?

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: In a deep crevice to the west of this village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm told that a statuette was removed from this temple.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Yes, that Saradominist priest brought a group of the villagers in here. Took it by force as far as I'm concerned. I'm not at all happy, standards have been slipping here for a while, but that was going too far.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: It's an insult to She!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: She! Elidinis! Goddess of growth and fertility! Lady of the River, Wife of Tumeken, Flower of the Desert. Mother of Icthlarin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ..Ah yes, that She. Well you're right, that would be an insult to Elidinis. In fact it seems that affront is what has brought the curse upon this village.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Well you know I suspected that all along, but does anyone listen to me anymore? No! Well I'm impressed you've come to the same conclusion as me, maybe I underestimated you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, why didn't you tell me about your suspicions when I was asking around about the curse then?

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: I have to apologise. We've already had one outsider around stirring trouble, which caused this whole mess in the first place. I feared that you were coming to force your ways on us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No I'm here to help.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: Well I'm grateful, anything you can do to find the statuette would be appreciated.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Good day to you young man.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Any progress on uncovering the town's curse yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes some actually. I managed to speak to the river spirits you told me about.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: You actually managed to find them? I'm impressed. As I say - no one has seen them in many many years.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes the spirits told me that the town is cursed because you guys have thrown away a statuette to the goddess Elidinis.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: Ah yes I heard a group led by that Saradominist priest went into the shrine and removed the Statuette. It all seemed a bit unnecessary to me. Didn't realise it would curse us though.

lines.speaker: Ghaslor the Elder
lines.text: I have no idea what they did with it afterwards.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I am a mighty and powerful genie. Why do you come here? Speak now or I'll have to think of something exceedingly nasty to do to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm after a statue that was thrown down here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ahh yes someone did drop a statue on my head recently. Very nice workmanship, looks quite old and valuable too. I'm surprised someone threw it away!

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I have accepted it as a gift as they clearly respect my terrible powers. They seek to keep me at bay by giving me valuables.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I can make a deal for the statuette?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Hmm a deal eh? I think we can come to some sort of arrangement. I'll give you the valuable statuette in return for, let me think ....

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: How about I give you the statue in exchange for the sole of the mayor of Nardah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't that a little harsh?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I wouldn't have said so. It seems a perfectly reasonable thing to request to me. I can think of many things a lot worse I could have asked for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I meant to remove someone's soul?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Oh that's easy. In my experience soles are very easy to remove with just a knife.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok I agree to the deal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I agreeing to that.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ah well it's your loss. No sole, then no statuette.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure they know you're down here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Silence cur! They fear and respect me and that's an end to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I can make a deal for the statuette?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a genie, grant me a wish.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Not all genies are goody two shoes who go round granting wishes. It's something that my pathetic brother does to make people like him, but that's not my style.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I like to think of myself more as an evil and scary sort of genie, who people talk about in hushed and fearful tones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you seem quite scary.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Why thank you, I put a lot of effort into my terrifyingness.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Wait! Did you say quite scary? I'll have you know I am VERY scary!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm yes that's what I meant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I must be off now.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Yes, run. Ruuuun in fear of.. The crevice genie of Nardah! Muhahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you sitting here in the dark?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Two reasons really. Firstly, I can see in the dark, so light or dark makes little difference to me.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Lastly, I gather it's normal for powerful terrifying creatures to lurk around in dark caves, so I found this one to lurk in myself, nice isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very pleasant, the snakes are a nice touch.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Why do I get the sense that I'm not being taken seriously here? Please try to remember I am very dangerous and not to be trifled with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I don't know about that. I can't have just anybody traipsing in here. I have a reputation to keep you see.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Actually I'll tell you what, you can hang around here for a bit, but be sure to spread the word about what a terrifying experience you had when you ran into... The crevice genie of Nardah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Ok I think I can manage that.

sections.title: Talking to the Genie again after accepting his deal

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Do you have the sole of Awusah, so that we can make our trade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Well no rush, I quite like the statuette too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it's not an easy thing to get.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Haha, even a pathetic human should be able to manage to get another human's sole.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Have you had any luck retrieving our statuette yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, well I have made some progress. I met a genie down the crevice. He told me that he would give me the statuette in exchange for your soul.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: What! That's just asking to much. So, he want's to have my soul when my time to die comes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well not exactly, he sort of basically said I should remove it with a knife.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: That's barbaric! I simply refuse. The price is too high. We'll just have to find other ways to get the town a good supply of water. Don't go bringing any knives near me.

sections.title: Using a knife on Awusah the Mayor

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: What do you think you are doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errm, I'm trying to remove your soul.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: What, have you lost your mind?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's for the good of the village. I met a genie down the crevice. He told me that he would give me the statuette in exchange for your sole.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: That's barbaric! We'll just have to find other ways to get the town a good supply of water. Keep your knife away from me please.

sections.title: Attempting to cut the mayor's shoes in his presence

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Hey, leave my shoes alone!

sections.title: Returning to the genie with the mayor's "sole"

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Do you have the sole of Awusah, so that we can make our trade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have it here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ah excellent, here's the statuette then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So um, why do you need part of Awusah's footwear anyway? I hope you're not going to do some nasty magic on his feet or something.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I hadn't thought of that. I just read that a lot of the most powerful beings are collecting soles these days. I haven't really got many yet though. You provided me with an opportunity to expand my collection.

sections.title: Talking to the genie after losing the statuette

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost my statuette.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Well fortunately for you, I am a mighty and powerful genie and managed to reacquire it. I will give it to you for another sole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have one here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ah excellent here's the statuette then.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: It's good doing business with you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will go and get one then.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Great! Another one for my collection.

sections.title: Talking to the mayor with the statuette

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Have you had any luck retrieving our statuette yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I finally got it. I had to destroy a pair of your shoes though. A crazy genie wanted a sole off your shoe in return for the statuette. I think he got souls and soles confused.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: I suppose sacrificing a pair of shoes for the greater good of the town is reasonable. I don't begin to understand the ways of genies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I thought it best not to correct him. He's somewhat less dangerous than he might be if he's a collector of shoe soles.

lines.speaker: Awusah the Mayor
lines.text: Anyway thanks for getting the statue back. You should take it to our temple and then we can see if our fountain starts working again.

sections.title: Talking to Shiratti with the statuette

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have got the statuette.

lines.speaker: Shiratti the Custodian
lines.text: That is great news! I could kiss you, but they don't allow shrine custodians to engage in that sort of behaviour. You should put the statuette back on its plinth.

sections.title: Using the statuette on the plinth
